comments.content:
1) I’ve had a rather weird experience: about a week ago one of my friends asked me for advice, since she knows I’m into the occult, and one of her friends has come across something which will supposedly bring up past life memories, she wanted to know what I thought about it. I immediately said it was a bad idea, since it’s impossible to say what would come up. The entire thing made me very uncomfortable and unsettled me a lot; there’s nothing in my background to suggest it should.

I also recalled about a story I thought was in the occult lore of someone who did just that as a teenager, discovered she’d done something absolutely horrible in the past, spent the rest of her life high out of her mind because she absolutely could not cope with what she did, and then killed herself shortly before her thirtieth birthday. The issue is that when I tried to place this, I drew an absolute blank. I had no idea where this story came from. I can’t even remember reading anything which could plausibly include such a story.

Things clicked when I read Grape Juice and Saltines’ post on the other blog: I found I could relate very well to it. I’ve had anger issues, terrible anxiety, and depression since I was a little kid, which journaling is revealing I’ve used to cover intense feelings of guilt and shame which I can’t seem to place. So, it’s possible they belong in a prior life, and at this point I’m starting to think that to be the case.

I also remember you discussing before how drug use in one life can screw up a future life’s nervous system, and I have a world class example of a dysfunctional nervous system. I also refused to drink any alcohol until my mid-20s; this despite the fact that most of my peers were into it as teenagers, and it’s legal to buy it from age 19 and up where I live. This wasn’t a taste thing either: I was concerned about what would happen if I drank, but I can’t for the life of me figure out why I would be so cautious about it.

This has me wondering: is it possible that the story didn’t come from anywhere in the occult lore, but rather what I was thinking of was my last life? I don’t remember anything about it, or any of my past lives, but it seems like it would fit.

2) If it’s possible this is a past life issue, do you have advice on how to deal with it? I’m doing basic Qabalistic spiritual work, and planning to do a fairly large amount of effort at transforming myself into a better person. Are there other things I can do to reduce how much of a karmic burden my future lives will have?

3) I'm assuming I can use divination here, but this is probably something best left for once I'm fairly confident with the art. I can't get consistent results on the day to day practice just yet, so I'm going to leave it for later. How long does it normally take to get good enough to be able to divine profitably on something major like this?

There seems to be a discussion over there and elsewhere, with multiple reports of people experiencing a sudden distancing from the gods: that the spiritual connections they had have either gone very silent or are seemingly not there anymore, and this was a fairly recent thing. Most of them do seem to be part of the radical progressive left (of course, it is Patheos Pagan), have allied with the Magical Resistance if they aren’t active members themselves, way too much of their blogging is taken up with Orange Man Bad, Cancel Culture is Awesome, Why Everyone is a White Supremacist and if You Think You’re Not that just Makes you Grand Wizard of the KKK, so on and so forth.

comments.title: Tooth Removal
comments.content: If one has a tooth removed, how long does the tooth retain some of its owner's etheric charge?  Do you know of any recommended cleaning or storage techniques for teeth once they've been removed?

replies.title: Re: Tooth Removal
replies.content: A very long time. Your best bet is to keep removed teeth somewhere safe. I don't know of any specific techniques; the teeth I've had removed are in little plastic bottles in a safe place.

replies.title: Another poster
replies.content:
I never thought of this. In the past few years I've had a couple of molars removed, in a dreadful clinic to which I will never return. On the second occasion the tooth broke into pieces, making the process especially painful. I was treated with callous indifference and disrespect throughout.

They didn't let me keep either tooth, although on the first occasion I requested to do so. Is there anything I can do to neutralize any bad juju that might arise from these stray teeth?

replies.title: Re: Another poster
replies.content: Unless they fall into the hands of someone who uses them for magical purposes, you should have no trouble. In any case, regular practice of a banishing ritual will keep you safe.

replies.title: Re: Another poster
replies.content:
It would be difficult for someone who had a grudge against you to get hold of your teeth, knowing who they belonged to, unless they worked in the clinic where the teeth were removed. They are typically disposed of as medical waste if there are no fillings-- which means they're burned at high temperatures. If there are fillings, you can't do that because it makes mercury vapor.

But just for future reference, you do have a right to keep the teeth if you want! When I had my wisdom teeth out, they were going to keep them, but I made a stink about it in the office, and insisted that if they kept them, they had to pay me for them by subtracting the value of the teeth from my bill (I paid cash). They handed over the teeth.

replies.title: Re: Tooth Removal
replies.content: Even if they hold some etheric residue why would it be important to hold on to and protect pulled out teeth?

replies.title: Re: Tooth Removal
replies.content: To keep someone from using them as magical links to you.

replies.content:

Joy Marie

replies.title: Re: Tooth Removal
replies.content: Bones and teeth are far more tenacious of etheric connection than other tissues. If you have an amputation, make sure the limb is cremated.

Gates:
East Raphael

Later I went trough an atheist life period, then agnostic and when I wanted to start the DH practice I was pretty shure there are Gods and Spirits out there, but I did not know any. I stuck with cristian themed names ever since, first with the names of God, now with the archangels. I obviously have a connection to the egregor.
2. a. What is your opinion on cristianity in this times?
2. b. Any advice for a druid/mage that is connected to roman catholicism.
2. c. A couple of Ogham readings have indicated, that I need to reconnect with the spirits of nature. How would a druid in my position do that?

Best regards,
V

replies.title: Re: Cristian druid practices
replies.content:
1) That seems perfectly sensible. There is no one set of appropriate names -- that's one of the beauties of the SoP; even within a single faith tradition such as Christianity, there are many appropriate forms. If that one works for you, go with it.

2a) My opinion? That it's not my religion, but a lot of people seem to find it very satisfactory to them, and I wish them blessings from their faith.

2b) If you haven't read it already, Meditations on the Tarot by an anonymous Catholic occultist might be worth your while.

2c) Pray to the archangel Sandalphon for guidance. In Christian occultism he is the archangel who guards and guides the planetary spirit of the Earth, and thus can lead you to the contacts with nature spirits that are right for you. You might also consider taking up a personal devotion to a saint noted for his or her closeness to nature, and ask the saint's help as well.

replies.title: Re: Way out of my depth...
replies.content:
"The tough question is which slide rule".. Not really IMO.

There is no one best layout, there are scores if not hundreds. General purpose slide-rules all contain more or less the same basic set of scales from this menu (I'll assume you know what the letters refer to): C & D, CI, DI, folded C and D scales, L, A, B, K, T, S, ST, R1&R2. The big dividing line is whether or not the rule has log-log scales, LL0, LL1, LL2, LL3 (and their inverses). Log-log scales are used to take arbitrary powers and roots, which is something an algebra student would want to be able to do; so I'd be sure to include those.

FWIW, my favorite is the Post 1461 pocket model and Post 1460 desk model. Also up there are the Pickett 600 and 500 models, which are respective equivalents. Faber-Castells have devoted fans as do the K&E's. The issue with reproducing ANY of these designs is copyright. I have no idea if the copyrights are still in force. I wouldn't want to find out the hard way, so I'd suggest coming up with one's own layout, which is not a big deal.

comments.title: "resealing the aura"
comments.content:
Well, I guessed wrong, I'd thought with all the other synchronicities I've had lately, you'd quote again from Sane Occultism. Alas, perhaps I'm not psychic after all ;-)

After little more than a week of rest after scaring myself silly, my divinations and..things... began prodding me back in the saddle again. I picked up Sane Occultism where I left off, which was to start Chapter XIV - Psychic Pathologies.

The description of the "untrained psychic" looked an awful lot like the "family curse" I've been stalking. Based on my experiment with "behave as though your fear is driving you 180 degrees in the wrong direction in interpreting what's going on", and the rather astounding results I got on that, it's a good working hypothesis. This may also explain why not studying the occult was an emphatic not-option for me this life, and I kept being thrust back to it, but why I've also been given very strict marching orders from Whomever on who, in the world of magic, to listen to and what each next step is, if it's based on re-establishing past life connections to certain Orders. It's like defusing a bomb. Ignore it and it goes boom. Cut the wrong wire and it goes boom. It does not turn off.

My questions are first theoretic, and then practical based on that:

1) Given the discussions we've been having about the polarities of the planes as they manifest in men and women, I hypothesize that if it is an untrained psychism that afflicts my family, it would then make somewhat sense of why the men tend to disintegrate, while the women merely become anxious or depressives. If being relatively receptive on the astral plane is a more normal state for women than men, perhaps that's why it really blows the men apart, as it cascades faster in imbalancing their health down the planes? Or is it just that the side effects of their own emotional outbursts in a culture that deals differently with male vs female anger creates a more damaging spiral in their interactions and what they perceive from others?

2) My son clearly has the "curse". He has reached the age of 7 (my daughter is 4). I only do the SOP and discursive meditation because of their ages and my own scrupulous fear of Doing Something Bad (Fortune says,"he is terribly afraid of black magic"), but I've caught, now that I've considered noticing, that many times if I've been I don't know how to describe... seeing an omen in something, he will seem to notice too. Like a cat. While nobody else does. He asks questions about what words mean that he seems to be picking up from undercurrents in the adults around him. I can't tell how much is that this is the age it starts, and how much is that I can accidentally prod him with my own leaking astral connections. To date we've just really focused on emotional coaching, those Virtues I mentioned last week, answering his questions as honestly as we can for age, and exercise and outside play like with any emotionally explosive child. Eating more meat than we'd been inclined to lately. Obviously, my own emotional self-regulation has been key. And I've created a habit of focusing on "shut it, shut it" and stroking my 'third eye' as though closing an eye if I catch the sensation of being too open near people, and making sure I keep up my housewifely and weightlifting habits. Are there other things I can do to make sure my untrained self doesn't "leak" or accidentally cause the rapport Fortune speaks of while I start to plod my way through what my divinations said was the safe next step (i.e. switching to the LRP and meditation topics of the Mysteries of Merlin)? Obviously, with my immediate family I can't sever all relations, though COVID has been a great cover for avoiding everyone else! I tried a red salt bag today, and I thought it helped, but I don't know if that's just a coincidence?

replies.title: Re: "resealing the aura"

1) Both of those are probably factors.

2) Certain kinds of psychism unquestionably run in families. As your son gets older he can learn to use meditation and magical practices to help turn down the volume on his psychism; simply knowing that the thoughts and feelings that are bombarding him aren't his will likely help a great deal -- though you'll have to be sure he knows not to talk about such things except with you. Most people can't deal with these matters at all.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Good! When you run into something like this that doesn't seem to make sense, it's always a good idea to wrestle with it, think about it, and see what it has to teach you.

In this case, if you'll reread the text of the ritual, the banishings in the four quarters don't banish only things coming from those four directions; they banish "all harmful influences and hostile magic, and every imbalance of the element of" whichever, irrespective of direction. Otherwise you'd be in trouble -- after all, there could be harmful influences coming from the southeast, or from north-northwest about 45° above the horizontal, or what have you. You don't banish with spirit because spirit is the integrative principle; it's the elements that limit and exclude.

Fasting is a significant practice in most forms of Christianity, and in Judaism as well. In the Christian tradition, the day for fasting is Friday.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
You might be interested in the brief account that Sallustius gives for this in chapter 6 of his treatise On the Gods and the World. Thomas Taylor's translation (which unfortuately turns the Greek names of the Gods into Roman Gods) is available in the public domain; A.D. Nock's translation can be borrowed for two weeks from archive.org.

In any case, Sallustius connects all the other Gods to one or another of the twelve Olympians, then sticks with the usual planetary assocations, with Apollon for the sun and Artemis for the moon, and Demeter for the planet Saturn, since Kronos isn't one of the twelve Olympians. The remaining five Olympian Gods are assigned to the elements; Athena gets aether.

comments.content: Could you say a few words about what Fortune referred to as desirelessness? Seems to connect to some parts of the Daodejing. Thank you!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Sure. Most people spend most of their time constantly wanting this thing to happen and wanting that other thing not to happen, and most of the things in question are completely out of their control. As the old Stoics pointed out, this is a very effective way to make yourself miserable and frustrated, because the universe doesn't notice what you want and wouldn't care if it did notice. The Stoic answer to that is to limit your desires to those things you can actually do something about, and let the universe proceed as it will anyway. That's what Fortune is talking about -- not cluttering up your mind and heart with useless desires -- and yes, it's also an important part of what Laozi was talking about.

comments.title: Dolmen Arch
comments.content:
Dear JMG

Having splurged on the over-the-top version of Dolmen Arch Vol I, I've been flicking though to 'get acquainted' while I decide on the spiritual symbols/powers I want to work with, and noticed some interesting parallels with some old Taoist practices.
I guess I should not be surprised as even with subjective approaches, we're living in the same sort of meat-sack and, energy, is, well, energy...

Anyway, just wanted to say that discovering the parallels is a very pleasant surprise - raises issues too of course (e.g. five elemental processes and correspondences), but pondering on this I'm starting to think it something not to get hung-up on as it is the underlying process that is important.

It is hard to put into words without considering this a lot more, but using cantrefs with emotional energy (virtues) almost seems like a druidical version of five elemental processes fundamental fusion of the 5 elements, while some of the Sphere of Protection (circulation of light) reminds me very much of more advanced Fusion exercises that deal with cleansing and 'sealing' of the aura and energy field.

The crystal skull of the lunar cauldron, skin and bone breathing - as well as the use of earth and sun (telluric and solar) are different but delightfully 'similar'.

In the system I studied, the use of cauldrons played a big part but, once again, it is a bit like meeting the brother or sister of an old friend - one feels like one knows them but they are different.

In my other practices, the earth connection is absolutely essential to provide grounding and avoid kundalini casualty style frying while the physical and energetic bodies are conditioned to be able to handle greater 'current'.

So, a bunny slope question - DA on P.19 point 2, the initial activation of the SoP 'you invoke your second power, the ray extends straight down through the midline of your body into the ground beneath you extending down all the way to the heart of the earth'

Read this and thought it must be a 'conceptual' visualisation at this stage of the material.

Then again, it is a different system so I should not assume or try to shoehorn other system ideas to a different system.

On DA P.107 Para 1 '...go deeper, into the heart of the living planet itself. Feel the immense power that drives earthquakes and volcanoes, lifts mountains toward the sky and moves entire continents... etc

I can see the sense in the description for 'infinite distance' when one is applying to / invoking deities; but at the same time my existing practices emphasised the need to 'wire-up' the baseline body and only introduce solar and earth currents slowly and carefully.

Perhaps I am reading too much into this, but am also mindful of having seen some of the ill-effects that can result from people trying to connect to too much energy too soon.

I will of course discover what is what as I work through the grades, but I am finding it challenging not to be influenced by the last 40 years of practice!
e.g. when I think of connecting to the earth it happens almost automatically but in a way that is only similar to (via bubbling springs points under feet), but not the same as the DA method.
Obviously I'll find out when I properly start on the DA course.

Those old tracks are deep but I have a strong feeling that I have much to learn and gain from taking my practice on a different path and using a different perspective.

The other thing that occurs to me - perhaps those old tracks have become a bit of a circular rut, and doing something like the DA will kick things into a spiral. I'm certainly enjoying what I have read so far - which bodes well for actual practice. :)

May I ask, do you have any thoughts/considerations on the matter?

Thank you.
[earthworm]

replies.title: Re: Dolmen Arch
replies.content: Most people who come to the Dolmen Arch don't have much in the way of serious magical or energetic training -- that's as true now as it was when the course was originally published -- and so the problem is one of getting any energy at all, rather than too much. Of course the visualizations will be mostly conceptual and abstract at first; the goal is to set an intention which will eventually bring through the power in the amounts that are needed. In your case, you'll want to be careful not to overload your energy body, but other than that you should have no trouble at all.

comments.title: Urim and Thummim
comments.content:
Dear JMG,

This is a response to Emmanuel G.'s question last week about the divination stones on the breast plate of the High Priest of Israel. He posted it late enough that it received no answer.

Emmanuel: Although most English translations of the Bible seem to forbid divination of all sorts, general rabbinical opinion is that the prohibition is only against necromancy and inquiring of the spirits of the dead (basically, what King Saul did when he went to the woman to inquire of the dead prophet Samuel.) I've asked a couple of rabbis about Tarot and geomancy and the like, and they have confirmed that as long as you're not summoning the dead, there is no prohibition.

About the Urim and Thummim, rabbinical thought is less clear. Particularly because there is no way to figure out exactly which gems or minerals they were made of (the language is ancient and ambiguous.)

Best regards,

Ms. Krieger

comments.title: Why seven?
comments.content:
Hello Mr. Greer!

First of all, thanks for providing this excellent service to the magic community.

I just joined a book club, and the first book being discussed is your Mystery Teachings from the Living Earth. We had a lively discussion about the introductory chapter as well as the first law, and during that discussion a question arose: Why seven laws and not some other number? The book says that the number is "fixed at seven for symbolic reasons". An assignment was given to each person to try to find an example of the number seven being used in a mystical context in other, older traditions, to try to get a sense of the meaning and importance of the number.

So, I challenged myself to find the oldest example of which there is written proof. During my research I came across the Corpus Hermeticum (and was amused but not entirely surprised to find that you wrote the introduction to a translation by one G.R.S. Mead), and in the first section there is a quote "And God-the-Mind, being male and female both... formed Seven Rulers that who enclose the cosmos that the sense perceives." This text (I think) is from the Gnostic period in approx. 100 - 300 AD, and to my mind, seems to represent the seven mutable stars that were known at that time - Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn.

That brings me to today: what was your particular reasoning for keeping the number of laws at precisely seven? Does it have something to do with the seven heavenly bodies? I suppose this question is open to all.

A follow-up question, which is more of a speculation that I'd like to share - let's say that Saturn or Mercury (the two dimmest Rulers) happened to be a little less bright in the sky and visible by unaided sight. Would mystics then have posited only Six Rulers, or is there something going on a little more hidden than "well there are X moving objects in the sky so let's base our list of principles on X"?

replies.title: Re: Why seven?
replies.content: The seven planets are the template for the whole series-of-seven thing. You can have quite a bit of fun figuring out which planet corresponds to which law. As for which came first, the planets or the number, that's a fine chicken-and-egg question to which I don't know the answer.

replies.title: Re: Mars Retrograde, Motivation
replies.content:
1) Reviewing your activities and animosities, and reconsidering which of them you'll continue once Mars turns around.

comments.title: Field report for Eating Habits
comments.content:
No specific question here, wanted to share an experience. The last two weeks I committed to working on better eating habits since it's problematic to me and worked on planes to provide resistance to my junk food urges. The last two discussions on CosDoc were really helpful and wanted to test it out.

I realized my urges to eat started with a thought in my mind. "pizza","cheese","fries" or any other junk food we typically find. With this thought, images of the food rush the mind and creates strong urges and I tend to think how it'd feel to be eating it right now and then proceed to order it. After eating it, guilt and uneasiness follows.

To beat the thought, the ideas I introduced were,"Eating junk is pleasurable to me","The momentary pleasures I get from eating junk isn't worth the long term effects". With this, I proceeded to flood images of me going about my daily life while eating healthy food. Following this, I meditated on the positive emotions that follow it and eventually being physically fit. The idea for me was to be as detailed as possible in my imagination. Midway through the experiment, I got pretty amazing results, because I always had a context for better eating habits no matter the situation for any particular day; the urges were significantly weakened.

This was really effective over short term.

- nomad

replies.title: Re: Field report for Eating Habits
replies.content: Excellent. That's a good constructive use of the toolkit.
